topic.name: Higher Education Financial Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving financial assistance and loan structures for students in higher education.

Document 1: Prohibits a four-year institution of higher education from being awarded, or continuing to receive funds for performance under, a federal grant or contract unless it offers its students at least six credit hours for the successful completion of courses offered by the Senior Reserve Officers' Training Corps (ROTC) program.
Requires an IHE to offer its students such credit hours even if successful completion of the ROTC courses occurs elsewhere.
Document 2: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to allow a consumer to request the removal of a previously reported default regarding a qualified education loan from a consumer report if the consumer meets the requirements of a private loan rehabilitation program.
Requires the Government Accountability Office to report on any challenges borrowers experience with the private loan rehabilitation program.
Document 3: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to appropriate for each fiscal year beginning with FY2016 the funding necessary to provide each eligible student with the maximum Pell Grant amount.
Converts the Pell Grant program into a mandatory spending program.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans.
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program.
Document 5: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to replace several existing federal student loan programs with a single repayment plan that caps annual debt repayments based on income.
Establishes the Income Dependent Education Assistance (IDEA) Loan Program and the IDEA Loan Repayment Program.
Document 6: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges on the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loans.
Sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans at the rate on high-yield 10-year Treasury notes plus 1%.
Document 7: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the 3.4% interest rate on Direct Stafford loans first disbursed to undergraduate students.
Modifies rules for required distributions from tax-exempt pension plans.

topic.name: Election and Labor Rights Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming election processes and labor rights in the United States.

Document 1: Restoring Integrity to America's Elections Act
amends the Federal Election Campaign Act with respect to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)
Document 2: Universal Right to Vote by Mail Act of 2009
prohibit a state from imposing additional conditions or requirements on the eligibility of an individual to cast a vote in federal elections by mail
Document 3: Fair Representation in Elections Act of 2011
requires the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative
Document 4: Employee Rights Act
makes it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation
Document 5: Fixing the Federal Voting Assistance Program Act of 2009
require the presidential designee responsible for carrying out federal functions under the Act to have experience in election administration
Document 6: Employee Paycheck Protection Act

requires a labor organization, before imposing, collecting, or increasing member or other covered employee dues or fees, to provide all employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement with written notice
Document 7: Democracy Day Act of 2014
designates Federal Election Day as a legal public holiday

topic.name: Energy Policy and Renewable Resources
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at promoting energy security, renewable energy sources, and the transition to electric and alternative fuel vehicles.

Document 1: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program.
award grants for preparation of a community deployment plans and programs
Document 2: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President the Office of Energy Security to oversee all federal energy security programs.
provides a tax credit for production of qualified flexible fuel motor vehicles
Document 3: authorize states of the Eastern interconnection and the Western Interconnection to establish interconnection-wide multistate transmission planning authorities
require the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to establish procedures governing MTA certification
Document 4: establish a standard that requires electric utilities to obtain an increasing percentage of their base quantity of electricity from renewable energy
Requires the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a renewable energy credit trading program
Document 5: impose a carbon pollution fee on any manufacturer, producer, or importer of a carbon polluting substance
provides financial support to state and local programs that assist communities in improving the resiliency of critical infrastructure
Document 6: require a retail electric supplier to submit to the Secretary of Energy (DOE) for calendar year 2013 and thereafter one or more of the following: specified federal renewable energy credits
Prescribes rules for federal renewable energy credit trading
Document 7: Amends provisions of the Internal Revenue Code relating to natural gas and alternative fuels, nuclear energy, and renewable energy
extend through December 31, 2019, the alternative fuel excise tax credit for fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas

topic.name: Medicare Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving the Medicare system and healthcare access in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to distribution of additional resident positions
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study the physician workforce.
Document 2: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare)
Requires a rebate agreement to require a drug or biological manufacturer to provide to the Secretary a rebate.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to collect data
Requires the Secretary to review data on the access of eligible individuals to immune globulins.
Document 4: Requires that medical services, hospital services, and other personal health services be made available to eligible individuals
Establishes the National Health Insurance Board in the Department of Health and Human Services.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to increase the Medicare physician payment update
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Medicare program should provide payments to physicians.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a demonstration project under title XVIII (Medicare)
Covers vaccines recommended for routine use by the Advisory Committee on Immunizations Practices.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish demonstration projects to increase public awareness about chronic kidney disease
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare coverage of kidney disease patient education services.

Document 2: Sex Crime Re-Entry Evaluation Act of 2010
impose on a defendant who has been convicted of a serious sex crime
Document 3: amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968
authorize the Department of Justice to make grants to state law enforcement agencies
Document 4: Prevention and Deterrence of Crimes Against Children Act of 2009
increase criminal penalties for offenses relating to the sexual exploitation of children
Document 5: Combat Methamphetamine Enhancement Act of 2008

training and technical assistance program to teach employees of state, local, and tribal law enforcement
Document 7: Transnational Criminal Organization Illicit Spotter Prevention and Elimination Act
make it a crime to transmit to another person the location, movement, or activities of law enforcement agents

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Foreign Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and influencing foreign policy concerning countries like Iran, Syria, and Iraq.

Document 1: U.S.-based property blocking and entry sanctions against any foreign person that threatens the peace, security, or stability of Iraq or Syria.
The government of Syria is in violation of numerous U.N. Security Council Resolutions regarding the provision of humanitarian assistance.
Document 2: Prohibits with respect to Iran the issuance of specified licenses to export or reexport civil aviation goods, services, or technology.
Imposes sanctions on a person that knowingly enters into an agreement with Iran to purchase or provide payment for future delivery of Iranian petroleum resources.
Document 3: U.S. policy that all diplomatic and coercive economic means should be used to compel the government of Bashar al Assad to halt the slaughter of the Syrian people.
Directs the President to impose specified entry and U.S.-based property sanctions against a foreign person that knowingly provided significant financial, material, or technological support to Syria.
Document 4: It is U.S. policy that Iran not be allowed to develop or acquire nuclear weapon capabilities.
The United States should continue to impose sanctions on Iran and its terrorist proxies.
Document 6: It shall be U.S. policy to fully implement and enforce sanctions against Iran, North Korea, and Syria for their proliferation activities and policies.

Directs the President to transmit to Congress a report identifying any foreign person who transferred to or acquired from Iran, Syria, or North Korea certain prohibited goods, services, or technology.

topic.name: Aviation and Transportation Safety
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and operational standards in aviation and transportation sectors.

Document 6: Establishes a dairy production margin protection program

requirements for rebuilding and replacing vessels

Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System

the amount of wages and self-employment income required for a quarter of coverage under the Social Security Act

tax-exempt educational organization

topic.name: Native American Land and Recognition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the recognition of Native American tribes and the management of tribal lands.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Reorganization Act to apply the Act to all federally recognized Indian tribes

Document 2: Amends the Gila Bend Indian Reservation Lands Replacement Act to prohibit class II or III gaming activities
Prohibits this gaming restriction from affecting decisions on lands taken into trust.

Document 6: Requires that 25,977 acres in Nevada be held in trust for the benefit of the Moapa Band of Paiutes
Gaming is restricted on this land.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts aimed at enhancing public services, community engagement, and governance efficiency.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to transfer funds appropriated for FY2009 for the National Endowment for the Humanities and the National Endowment for the Arts to the Department of Veterans Affairs for the enhancement of services for veterans at community-based outpatient clinics.
Enhancement of services for veterans at community-based outpatient clinics.
Document 2: Establishes the United States Commission on an Open Society with Security to study how the government may provide for both security in, and public access to, federal buildings and other federal property and sites.
Document 3: Requires the Postal Service to ensure that every post office has access to running water and bathroom facilities.
Prohibits the Post Office from considering whether the post office meets the requirements of this Act when making closure determinations.
Document 4: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish a publicly available website for tracking, in detail, all expenditures of funds over $500 which are appropriated or otherwise available to the federal government.
Document 5: Reduces by 10% the amounts authorized to be appropriated to the Department of Defense (DOD) for FY2012 for printing and reproduction.
Applies to Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and defense-wide operation and maintenance funds.
Document 6: Establishes the History Is Learned from the Living grant program.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to make one-year grants of $200,000 each to community groups, organizations, or institutions.
Document 7: Revises membership requirements for and reduces to nine the number of members on the board of directors of the Metropolitan Washington Airports Authority.
Limits members to one term.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions and credit practices.

Document 1: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to authorize the issuance of remotely created checks only if the consumer has designated in writing a person to issue them.
Empowers the CFPB to investigate violations of this Act.
Document 2: Prohibits a consumer reporting agency from making any consumer report containing information related to a medical debt if the date on which the debt was placed for collection is less than 180 days.
The debt collector may not communicate with or report any information to a consumer reporting agency concerning such a debt during the 180-day period.
Document 3: Excludes from computation of debts the secured or unsecured portions of debts secured by the debtor's principal residence if the current value of that residence is less than the secured debt limit.
Requires the court to disallow a claim that is subject to any remedy for damages or rescission due to violations of state or federal consumer protection law.
Document 4: Retains states' regulatory authority over insurance-related consumer protection and unfair trade practices.
Requires NARAB to receive and investigate consumer complaints, maintaining a toll-free telephone number.
Document 5: Prohibits a person from extending any consumer credit through the use by the consumer of a check or other negotiable instrument sent by the creditor to solicit an extension of consumer credit, unless the consumer has requested it in writing.
Shields a consumer from liability for any interest, fee, or penalty charged in connection with such negotiable instrument.
Document 6: Extends consumer protections to loyalty, award, or promotional gift cards by including them within the definitions of 'general-use prepaid card' and 'gift certificate.'
Makes it unlawful for a person that has filed for federal bankruptcy protection to either sell, resell, issue, or reissue a gift certificate or store gift card.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act regarding disclosures required with each billing cycle under an open end consumer credit plan.
Prescribes additional statements disclosing the length of time it will take to pay off the balance if only the minimum amount due is paid.

Document 3: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to require a registered lobbyist's semiannual disclosure report
to include the name, address, and principal place of business of the other person or entity that retained the subcontractor.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nations
to domestic corporations with respect to which one or more foreign principals serves on the board of directors.
Document 5: Revises certain requirements for anti-money laundering programs with respect to federally insured depository institutions
Sets forth civil and criminal penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 6: Terminates any provision of federal law that subjects a consumer to a penalty for withdrawing or transferring funds
Requires such entities to establish a procedure for notifying customers of their right to elect to receive statements of account.
Document 7: Amends the federal judicial code to provide that the statute of limitations applicable for commencing actions by certain terrorism victims
is an affirmative defense that is waived by such foreign state or employee if not timely raised in a responsive pleading.

topic.name: Transportation and Infrastructure Legislation

Document 2: Repeals provisions exempting the Transportation Security Administration from specified federal procurement requirements.
Document 4: Establishes the Emergency Transportation Safety Fund.

topic.name: Homeland Security and Transportation Safety

Document 7: directs the federal government to conduct cybersecurity activities to provide shared situational awareness

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Policy

Document 1: establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
redefines 'immediate relative' to mean a child, spouse, permanent partner, or parent of a U.S. citizen
